elements.title: Miloš Vučević
elements.contents: ... that Miloš Vučević (pictured) served in the National Assembly of Serbia for only five days?

elements.title: Robert N. Mullin
elements.contents: ... that historian Robert N. Mullin, in addition to being an expert on outlaw Billy the Kid, was once a politician who was responsible for the building of El Paso Airport?

elements.title: Miku
elements.contents: ... that the guitarist who created the song "Miku" often felt like he was talking to the fictional Hatsune Miku?

elements.title: Setkya Dewi
elements.contents: ... that Setkya Dewi, chief queen of Burma, studied Western astronomy and English and was once gifted a telescope by a British envoy because of her interest in science?

elements.title: New Pokémon Snap
elements.contents: ... that players can print out their photographs taken in the video game New Pokémon Snap?

elements.title: Bambang Trisnohadi
elements.contents: ... that Bambang Trisnohadi and Lucky Avianto were both nicknamed the "hat-trick general" for being the best graduate of three different military institutions?

elements.title: unincorporated communities in Wisconsin
elements.contents: ... that the Wisconsin State Cartographer's Office has been inventorying unincorporated communities in Wisconsin to find "cartographic phantoms"?

elements.title: Heraclitus
elements.contents: ... that the story of the philosopher Heraclitus dying while covered in dung may have been a parody of his beliefs?
